dialogueStructure.catalyst: Janaka asks three fundamental questions: How to attain ultimate knowledge, how to find detachment, and how to claim liberation.

coreTeachings.title: Effortless Awareness
coreTeachings.description: True liberation requires zero difficult physical penance, complex yoga positions, or rigid rituals. Transformation happens instantly simply by listening to absolute truth (Shravanamatrena) and shifting from a 'doer' to an unaffected 'witness'.

coreTeachings.title: The Dream World
coreTeachings.description: The material objective world is essentially an illusion. True realization is waking up from the dream-state of limitation and resting in the absolute oneness of the inner divinity of the Self.

coreTeachings.title: Transcending the Mind
coreTeachings.description: You are not your biological body, your emotional mind, or your psychological ego. You are the infinite field of pure consciousness—forever free, untouched, and fundamentally blissful.

oshoCommentary.coreInsight: Osho highlights Ashtavakra's verses as a direct, confrontational path. There are no steps to enlightenment because your ultimate nature cannot be built—it can only be remembered.
